appraisal_events.evidence: « …où baron de Charlus passait pour un vieux domestique ruiné… » ; « …jadis si fier, maintenant tout timide — dans des accès de vrai désespoir. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator shows that, during his outings with Morel, Charlus is publicly taken for a ruined servant and, through his kindnesses to Morel that are misunderstood, sees his assurance turn into timidity and despair. This locally lowers his social and affective dignity.

status_effects.explanation: Taken for an old servant and dependent on the other to pay, he undergoes a diminishment of status in the scene described.
The narrator insists on his transformation « jadis si fier, maintenant tout timide » and his « accès de vrai désespoir », marking a strong drop in affective position.
